_note: Frontend-only UX layer — no backend endpoint. Mood selection lives in local UI state (localStorage) until backend persistence ships (future backlog). Each mood reskins the WHOLE shell via [data-mood] tokens, including a dedicated --sidebar-*/--topbar-* chrome layer.

moods.label: Comfort
moods.description: Balanced, premium and friendly. The default.

moods.label: Focus
moods.description: Denser surfaces and stronger hierarchy for deep operational work.
moods.chrome: charcoal sidebar, grayer field, tighter rows

moods.label: Calm
moods.description: Softer contrast for long or sensitive sessions (clinical, tax, accounting).
moods.chrome: light sage sidebar, gentle teal accent

moods.label: High contrast
moods.description: Accessibility-first contrast, strong borders and visible focus.
moods.chrome: white sidebar, strong black borders, no shadows

moods.label: Night
moods.description: Low-glare dark shell, not a flat inversion.
moods.chrome: full dark shell, sidebar darker than panels
